transliteration:
kleśo ’dhikataras teṣām avyaktāsakta-cetasām
avyaktā hi gatir duḥkhaṁ dehavadbhir avāpyate
literal_meaning: Greater is the trouble of those whose minds are attached to the Unmanifest; for the path of the Unmanifest is hard for embodied beings.
contextual_meaning: Krishna warns that trying to relate to an abstract, formless Absolute triggers extra cognitive-emotional strain because the human nervous system is wired for concrete, relational experience.
psychological_state: Attempting to suppress sensory and relational needs in favor of pure abstraction produces chronic low-grade stress and self-doubt.
trigger_scenario: A high-achieving professional tries to meditate on “emptiness” to escape burnout, but ends up more anxious because there is no felt sense of safety to hold the experience.
anchor_text: Start with a form you can feel, then let it dissolve.
